note: Declarative theorem -> 01e-property mapping (Stage C of the impl plan). Tune these entries to match the bench-rq2a-20260508-speca 01e granularity WITHOUT recompiling Lean. Emitted core fields follow the benchmark vocabulary: type=invariant, severity CRITICAL|HIGH|MEDIUM, exploitability external-attack|local-attack, entry_points from {CallbackHandler,FunctionCall,ProgramEntry,Initialization}. Fields prefixed with x_ are documentation for tuning and are NOT emitted. `theorem` must be the fully-qualified Lean name so `speca-export` can resolve it; `covers_hint` is matched against 01b subgraph element ids by the driver; `bug_bounty_area` is checked against BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE.json.

properties.text: A validator must never have two attestations signed for the same target epoch with distinct targets
properties.assertion: forall v, e: count(distinct target_root signed by v with target_epoch = e) <= 1
properties.x_exploitability_note: Requires a validator signing key. A client that fails to detect S1 equivocation lets a Byzantine validator commit to two histories without being slashed, weakening accountable safety.

properties.text: A validator must never cast an attestation whose source-target span surrounds an earlier one of its own
properties.assertion: forall v, a1, a2 by v: not (source(a1) < source(a2) and target(a2) < target(a1))
properties.x_exploitability_note: Requires a validator signing key. Missing S2 detection permits surround voting that can finalize conflicting checkpoints.

properties.text: Two conflicting finalized checkpoints must imply a slashable two-thirds quorum intersection
properties.assertion: forall st: finalization_fork(st) -> forall v in inter(qL, qR): violates_S1_or_S2(v, st)
properties.x_exploitability_note: A safety violation (two conflicting finalized checkpoints) is the strongest consensus failure. This property is the accountability guarantee; a client bug that finalizes a fork without the slashable-intersection invariant breaks it.

properties.text: Accountable safety must hold for k-finalized checkpoints under dynamic validator sets
properties.assertion: forall st, k: k_finalization_fork(st, k) -> exists w: q_intersection_slashed_witness(st, w)
properties.x_exploitability_note: Generalizes accountable safety from 1-finalization to arbitrary k-finalization and from static to dynamic validator sets; covers finality-with-churn edge cases a client may mishandle.

properties.text: Quorum-intersection weight accounting must stay positive under validator activation and exit churn
properties.x_exploitability_note: Incorrect quorum-weight arithmetic (overflow, churn double-count) can misjudge whether a 2/3 threshold is met, affecting justification/finalization decisions.

properties.text: Justification status must match the bounded supermajority-link chain rule with no unbounded recursion
properties.assertion: forall c: justified(c) <-> exists bounded chain of supermajority links from anchor to c
properties.x_exploitability_note: A justification computation that diverges from the bounded-chain rule can justify/finalize incorrectly, but requires control over checkpoint structure rather than a single crafted message.

properties.text: With two-thirds honest stake the chain must always be able to finalize a new checkpoint without new slashing
properties.assertion: two_thirds_good(st) -> exists st': extends(st, st') and finalized(st') and no_new_slashed(st, st')
properties.x_exploitability_note: Liveness property. Typically outside a client bug-bounty's safety scope; flagged bug_bounty_eligible=false unless the scope explicitly includes liveness.
